writing.welcome.title: Welcome to OET Writing
writing.welcome.subtitle: Five stages take you from baseline diagnostic to exam-day mastery.
writing.welcome.hero.title: Welcome — let's set up your Writing pathway
writing.welcome.hero.description: Five stages take you from baseline diagnostic to exam-day mastery, with daily plans, AI coach hints, and Dr Ahmed's canon at every step.
writing.welcome.pathway.heading: The 5-stage pathway
writing.welcome.pathway.subtitle: Each stage unlocks the next once you meet the readiness checks. The diagnostic builds your personal plan.
writing.welcome.cta.heading: Ready when you are
writing.welcome.cta.subtitle: We need a few quick details to choose your diagnostic letter and tailor your daily plan.
writing.welcome.stages.onboardingDescrip: Tell us your profession, target band, exam date, and weekly time.
writing.welcome.stages.diagnosticDescrip: A timed 50-minute letter to baseline your six criteria.
writing.welcome.stages.practiceDescripti: Daily letters, coach hints, exemplar comparison, revisions.
writing.welcome.stages.masteryDescriptio: Mocks, tutor reviews, and exam-day readiness.
writing.hub.hero.title: Practise OET Writing your way
writing.hub.hero.description: Start a timed mock, work through targeted practice scenarios, or take the diagnostic to build your plan.
writing.hub.start.title: Choose how you want to practise
writing.hub.start.description: Pick a full mock, a focused practice scenario, or the diagnostic.
writing.hub.cards.mocks.title: Mock exams
writing.hub.cards.mocks.description: Sit a full, timed OET Writing letter under exam conditions — on computer or printable paper.
writing.hub.cards.practice.title: Practice library
writing.hub.cards.practice.description: Work through scenario-based letters filtered by profession, letter type, and difficulty.
writing.hub.cards.diagnostic.title: Diagnostic
writing.hub.cards.diagnostic.description: Take a baseline writing assessment so we can tailor your pathway and target your weakest criteria.
writing.hub.resources.title: Sharpen your technique
writing.hub.resources.description: Study model answers, learn how letters are scored, and review your past submissions.
writing.hub.cards.model.title: Model answers
writing.hub.cards.model.description: See high-scoring example letters with rationale for every paragraph and criterion.
writing.hub.cards.rulebook.title: Rulebook
writing.hub.cards.rulebook.description: The exact criteria the checker applies to your draft — Purpose, Content, Conciseness, Genre, Organisation, and Language.
writing.hub.cards.submissions.title: Past submissions
writing.hub.cards.submissions.descriptio: Revisit your graded letters, feedback, and tutor reviews across every subtest.
writing.profileSetup.profession.title: Tell us who you are
writing.profileSetup.profession.subtitle: Your profession decides letter types we drill and scenarios we surface.
writing.profileSetup.goals.title: Set your target and weekly budget
writing.profileSetup.focus.title: What letter types do you need most?
writing.profileSetup.focus.subtitle: Pick at least two.
writing.profileSetup.confirm.title: Review and confirm
writing.diagnostic.briefing.title: Diagnostic Assessment
writing.diagnostic.briefing.subtitle: A 50-minute baseline of your six writing criteria.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.hero.title: A 50-minute baseline of your six writing criteria
writing.diagnostic.briefing.hero.descrip: One letter under real exam timing. Your score builds your personal pathway and seeds your daily plan.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.format.subti: You'll get one timed letter task in your chosen profession. The format mirrors the real OET writing exam.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.timeline.rea: Read the case notes (Case Notes). Editor is locked, copy-paste disabled.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.timeline.wri: Write a 180-220 word letter. Spell-check ON, coach OFF.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.timeline.gra: AI rubric + canon engine score across six criteria.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.strict.title: Strict mode reminders
writing.diagnostic.briefing.strict.item1: Reading and writing windows cannot be paused.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.strict.item2: Stay in the browser tab — switching is allowed but the timer keeps running.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.strict.item3: If you reload the page mid-attempt, your progress is restored from the server.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.ready.title: Ready to begin?
writing.diagnostic.briefing.ready.subtit: You can pause before you click Start — once the timer begins, the credit is used.
writing.diagnostic.briefing.error.profil: Could not load your profile. Complete onboarding first.
writing.diagnostic.session.placeholderRe: The writing window opens after 5 minutes of reading.
writing.diagnostic.session.helper.notSta: You can submit once the writing window starts.
writing.diagnostic.session.helper.tooSho: Letter is too short to submit (50 word minimum).
writing.diagnostic.session.helper.ready: You can submit any time during the writing window.
writing.diagnostic.session.error.startWr: Could not start the writing window.
writing.diagnostic.results.title: Your diagnostic result
writing.diagnostic.results.hero.descript: The same six-criterion rubric used in the live exam. Each score is paired with rule-cited feedback so you know exactly what to fix.
writing.diagnostic.results.criteria.head: Criteria radar
writing.diagnostic.results.criteria.subt: Where you sit against a target B-band profile.
writing.diagnostic.results.canon.heading: Canon violations ({count})
writing.diagnostic.results.canon.subtitl: Dr Ahmed's style canon — fix these first; they hold back C4 and C6.
writing.diagnostic.results.exemplar.head: Side-by-side with a gold-standard exemplar
writing.diagnostic.results.exemplar.subt: Word-level differences are highlighted; hover an annotation marker to see the rule.
writing.diagnostic.results.cta.heading: Start your pathway
writing.diagnostic.results.cta.subtitle: Today's plan is ready — first item picks up from your weakest criterion.
writing.skillTree.title: W1-W8 — the eight skills every OET letter rests on
writing.skillTree.hero.description: Each node holds two micro-lessons. Pass the quiz at ≥80% to mark it complete.
writing.skillTree.section.title: Pick a sub-skill to drill into
writing.skillTree.section.description: Mastery percentages reflect your last 20 letters; lessons are independent and stay open.
writing.skillTree.list.label: Writing skill tree
writing.skillTree.error.load: Could not load the skill tree.
writing.lessons.catalogue.title: Writing micro-lessons
writing.lessons.catalogue.description: Five-to-ten minute lessons grouped by sub-skill. Each ends with a 5-question quiz.
writing.lessons.filter.title: By sub-skill
writing.lessons.filter.description: Click a skill to focus the list. Empty filter shows all lessons.
writing.lessons.filter.label: Filter lessons by sub-skill
writing.lessons.list.label: Writing lessons
writing.lessons.list.empty: No lessons match the filter yet.
writing.lessons.detail.error.load: Could not load this Writing lesson.
writing.lessons.detail.lesson.title: Foundation notes
writing.lessons.detail.drill.title: Apply the skill
writing.lessons.detail.quiz.title: Score check
writing.practice.library.title: Browse the scenario library
writing.practice.library.description: Choose any scenario, then pick practice or coached mode. Letters are graded the same way the diagnostic was.
writing.practice.library.filters.title: Narrow the catalogue
writing.practice.library.filters.descrip: Filters apply server-side; topic chips reflect the current results.
writing.practice.library.topics.label: Topics in results:
writing.practice.library.list.label: Writing scenarios
writing.practice.session.helper.tooShort: Letter is too short to submit ({remaining} words to minimum).
writing.practice.session.helper.ready: When you submit, the rubric, canon engine, and exemplar matcher run in parallel.
writing.submissions.detail.title: Submission
writing.submissions.detail.heroDescripti: Read the letter you sent and continue to the result or revise it.
writing.submissions.grading.title: Sit tight while we grade your letter
writing.submissions.grading.description: The pipeline runs in under a minute on average. You'll be redirected automatically when it finishes.
writing.submissions.grading.polling: Switched to polling — checking every 5 seconds.
writing.submissions.results.title: Submission results
writing.submissions.results.description: Six-criterion rubric, canon engine, and the closest exemplar — together they explain exactly what to change next.
writing.submissions.results.next.heading: What's next?
writing.submissions.results.next.descrip: Take one of these actions — each is recorded against this submission.
writing.submissions.results.actions.appe: Briefly explain why you believe the grade is wrong (20+ characters). We'll re-grade with a higher-cost model.
Appeal cancelled — at least 20 characters required.
Appeal submitted. Result will update once reviewed.
writing.submissions.results.actions.tuto: Tutor review requested. You will be notified when a tutor returns it.
writing.submissions.results.actions.show: Letter published anonymously to the showcase.
writing.submissions.results.actions.disp: Why is this detection wrong? (10+ characters)
writing.submissions.revise.title: Revise letter
writing.submissions.revise.editorPlaceho: Edit your original letter to address the feedback above.
writing.submissions.revise.helper.tooSho: Letter is too short to submit.
writing.submissions.revise.helper.noChan: Make at least one change before resubmitting.
writing.submissions.revise.helper.ready: Your revision will be graded separately and linked to the original.
writing.caseNoteDrills.title: Case-note drills
writing.canon.library.hero.title: Dr Ahmed's Writing rules in one place
writing.canon.library.hero.description: The first pathway release exposes the launch canon and your recent rule-violation history without exposing grading prompts or model internals.
writing.canon.library.browse.title: Find a rule
writing.canon.library.browse.description: Filter by text, category, rule id, or severity.
writing.canon.library.error.load: Could not load the Writing canon.
writing.canon.detail.examples.title: Examples
writing.canon.detail.lessonPrompt: There's a linked micro-lesson for this rule.
writing.canon.detail.history.heading: Your personal history
writing.canon.detail.history.summary: You've triggered this rule {count, plural, one {# time} other {# times}} across your recent letters.
writing.canon.detail.error.load: Could not load this canon rule.
writing.mistakes.library.title: The 20 mistakes that cost the most marks
writing.mistakes.library.description: Every card has a wrong/right example pair and links straight to the canon rule and lesson.
writing.mistakes.library.list.label: Common mistakes
writing.mistakes.library.cta.mine: See mistakes from your own letters
writing.mistakes.mine.title: The mistakes showing up most in your letters
writing.mistakes.mine.description: Tracks across the last 20 graded letters. Tap a card to read the lesson and the canon rule.
writing.mistakes.mine.top5.heading: Top 5 to fix this week
writing.mistakes.mine.top5.subtitle: Knock these down and your next letter's C4 and C6 will jump.
writing.mistakes.mine.empty.body: You don't have any tracked mistakes yet. Submit a letter and the system will start surfacing the patterns it catches.
writing.mistakes.mine.all.heading: Every mistake we've tracked
writing.mocks.catalogue.title: Mocks under strict exam conditions
writing.mocks.catalogue.description: No coach, no spell-check, no pause. A mock is the single best predictor of your exam-day band.
writing.mocks.catalogue.before.title: Before you start:
writing.mocks.catalogue.before.body: mocks reflect real exam conditions. Find a quiet 50-minute slot. Closing the browser mid-mock counts as abandoned.
writing.mocks.catalogue.list.label: Mock catalogue
Writing window opens after the reading window.
writing.mocks.session.helper.notStarted: Submit unlocks at the start of the writing window.
writing.mocks.session.helper.tooShort: Letter is too short to submit.
writing.mocks.session.error.startWriting: Could not start the writing window.
writing.mocks.results.title: Mock results
writing.mocks.results.description: Strict-mode result: this is the closest signal to your real exam-day band.
writing.mocks.results.criteria.heading: Criteria radar
writing.mocks.results.trajectory.heading: Mock band trajectory
writing.mocks.results.canon.heading: Canon violations
writing.mocks.results.priorities.heading: Top three priorities for next mock
writing.mocks.results.next.heading: What's next?
writing.mocks.results.next.description: Review priorities or jump back to your daily plan.
writing.mocks.results.statusGrading: Mock grading is still finishing. This page will refresh automatically.
writing.mocks.results.awaiting.title: Awaiting examiner marking
writing.mocks.results.awaiting.body: Your mock Writing letter has been sent to a human examiner. Mock Writing is never graded by AI — your band and feedback will appear here once an examiner marks it. This page refreshes automatically.
writing.stats.title: Track every dimension of your writing progress
writing.stats.description: Pull a single dashboard view of bands, criteria, letter types, canon, time, sub-skill mastery, readiness, and activity.
writing.stats.bands.title: Raw score over time
writing.stats.bands.description: The trend line is least-squares regression across your submissions.
writing.stats.criteria.heading: Criteria — current vs target
writing.stats.letterTypes.heading: Letter type performance
writing.stats.canon.heading: Canon violations — top hits
writing.stats.time.heading: Time management
writing.stats.skills.heading: Sub-skill mastery
writing.stats.calendar.heading: Activity heatmap
writing.stats.calendar.subtitle: One square per day; darker means more submissions or drills.
writing.stats.calendar.label: Activity heatmap
writing.stats.error.load: Could not load all stats panels.
writing.showcase.title: Browse A-grade letters from other learners
writing.showcase.description: Anonymised, opt-in showcase posts — see how peers solved the same letter types.
writing.showcase.list.label: Showcase posts
writing.showcase.list.empty: No showcase posts yet for this filter.
writing.tools.paraphrase.title: Paraphrase a sentence three ways
writing.tools.paraphrase.description: Get three alternative phrasings at different formality levels. Useful when a sentence sounds wrong but you can't say why.
writing.tools.paraphrase.inputPlaceholde: e.g. The patient was admitted with chest pain and was discharged after two days.
writing.tools.ask.title: Chat with the coach about a past submission
writing.tools.ask.description: Paste a submission ID, then ask anything — why was C4 low, how would Dr Ahmed open this letter, etc.
writing.tools.ask.empty: Ask something to begin. For example: "Why did C4 drop on this letter?"
writing.tools.ask.placeholder: Type a question about the letter…
writing.tools.ask.error.pickFirst: Pick a submission first so the assistant has your letter to read.
writing.today.title: Today's Writing plan
writing.today.hero.description: Adaptive plan built from your pathway, weakness signal, and last-graded letters.
writing.today.section.description: Tap Start to open the workspace. Mark complete when finished.
writing.paper.readingLock: Reading window: study the booklet. The answer booklet unlocks when writing time begins.
writing.paper.submittedBody: Your letter has been submitted. A tutor will review it; your booklet is now locked.
writing.paper.cover.instruction1: You must write your answer in the answer booklet.
writing.paper.cover.instruction2: You have 5 minutes to read the case notes. You cannot write during this time.
writing.paper.cover.instruction3: You then have 40 minutes to write your letter.
writing.paper.helper.reading: Reading window — the answer booklet is locked.
writing.paper.helper.tooShort: Keep writing — at least {min} words before you can submit.
writing.paper.error.load: We could not load this paper session.
writing.paper.error.startWriting: We could not start the writing window.
writing.paper.error.submit: We could not submit your letter. Please try again.
